revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

{{神社| 名称=角宮神社| 所在地=京都府長岡京市井ノ内南内畑35| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|31|N|135|41|20|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=火雷神| 社格=式内社（名神大）・村社| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[5月1日]]}}
'''角宮神社'''（すみのみやじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[長岡京市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[式内社|式内]][[名神大社]]「[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]] 乙訓坐大雷神社」の論社である（他の論社は[[向日神社]]（[[向日市]]）に合祀された「火雷神社」）。
==祭神== 火雷神（ほのいかづちのかみ）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[タマヨリビメ|玉依姫命]]・[[賀茂建角身命|建角身命]]・[[垂仁天皇|活目入彦五十狹茅尊]]・[[春日神]]を配祀する。「乙訓坐火雷神」については『[[山城国風土記]]』の[[賀茂神社]]縁起で、賀茂建角身命の子の玉依姫命が、火雷神が化身した丹塗矢によって懐妊し、賀茂別雷命が生まれたと記されている。賀茂建角身命と玉依姫命は[[賀茂御祖神社|下鴨神社]]の、賀茂別雷命は[[賀茂別雷神社|上賀茂神社]]の祭神である。
==歴史== 国史の初見は『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）7月8日条で、その後も[[祈雨]]の神としてたびたび朝廷からの奉幣があった。
[[承久]]3年（[[1221年]]）の[[承久の乱]]で焼失し、長らく復興されないままになっていた。[[文明 (日本)|文明]]16年（[[1484年]]）、現在地に再興され、井ノ内の[[産土神]]とされた。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格した。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/yamashiro/html/020104-02.html 角宮神社]（延喜式神社の調査）
[[Category:京都府の神社|すみのみやしんしや]] [[Category:名神大社|すみのみやしんしや]] [[Category:村社|すみのみやしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

==歴史== 国史の初見は『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）7月8日条で、その後も[[祈雨]]の神としてたびたび朝廷からの奉幣があった。

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=角宮神社| 画像=[[File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG|250px]]| 所在地=京都府長岡京市井ノ内南内畑35| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|31|N|135|41|20|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=火雷神| 社格=式内社（名神大）・村社| 本殿=[[神明造]]| 例祭=[[5月1日]]}}
'''角宮神社'''（すみのみやじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[長岡京市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内]][[名神大社]]「[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]] 乙訓坐大雷神社」の論社である（他の論社は[[向日神社]]（[[向日市]]）に合祀された「火雷神社」）。
==祭神== 火雷神（ほのいかづちのかみ）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[タマヨリビメ|玉依姫命]]・[[賀茂建角身命|建角身命]]・[[垂仁天皇|活目入彦五十狹茅尊]]・[[春日神]]を配祀する。
「乙訓坐火雷神」については『[[山城国風土記]]』の[[賀茂神社]]縁起で、賀茂建角身命の子の玉依姫命が、火雷神が化身した丹塗矢によって懐妊し、[[賀茂別雷命]]が生まれたと記されている。賀茂建角身命と玉依姫命は[[賀茂御祖神社|下鴨神社]]の、賀茂別雷命は[[賀茂別雷神社|上賀茂神社]]の祭神である。
==歴史== 国史の初見は『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）7月8日条で、その後も[[祈雨]]の神としてたびたび朝廷からの奉幣があった。
[[承久]]3年（[[1221年]]）の[[承久の乱]]で焼失し、長らく復興されないままになっていた。[[文明 (日本)|文明]]16年（[[1484年]]）、現在地に再興され、井ノ内の[[産土神]]とされた。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格した。
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみのみやしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名すみのみやしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:長岡京市]] {{shinto-stub}}

「乙訓坐火雷神」については『[[山城国風土記]]』の[[賀茂神社]]縁起で、賀茂建角身命の子の玉依姫命が、火雷神が化身した丹塗矢によって懐妊し、[[賀茂別雷命]]が生まれたと記されている。賀茂建角身命と玉依姫命は[[賀茂御祖神社|下鴨神社]]の、賀茂別雷命は[[賀茂別雷神社|上賀茂神社]]の祭神である。
==歴史== 国史の初見は『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）7月8日条で、その後も[[祈雨]]の神としてたびたび朝廷からの奉幣があった。
[[承久]]3年（[[1221年]]）の[[承久の乱]]で焼失し、長らく復興されないままになっていた。[[文明 (日本)|文明]]16年（[[1484年]]）、現在地に再興され、井ノ内の[[産土神]]とされた。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格した。

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shrine | name = Suminomiya Shrine | image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamayorihime no Mikoto|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, and {{ill|Kasuga Kami|ja|春日神}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamayorihime no Mikoto|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, and {{ill|Kasuga Kami|ja|春日神}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] {{shinto-stub}} [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamayorihime no Mikoto|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, and {{ill|Kasuga Kami|ja|春日神}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamayorihime no Mikoto|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, and {{ill|Kasuga Kami|ja|春日神}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年11月10日 (金) 12:13 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=角宮神社 |画像=[[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿（左）・本殿覆屋（右奥） |所在地=[[京都府]][[長岡京市]]井ノ内南内畑35 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[火雷大神|火雷神]] |社格=[[式内社]]（名神大）・旧[[村社]] |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭=[[5月1日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=角宮神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''角宮神社'''（すみのみやじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[長岡京市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内]][[名神大社]]「[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]] 乙訓坐大雷神社」の論社である（他の論社は[[向日神社]]（[[向日市]]）に合祀された「火雷神社」）。 == 祭神 == [[火雷大神|火雷神]]（ほのいかづちのかみ）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[玉櫛媛|玉依姫命]]・[[賀茂建角身命|建角身命]]・[[垂仁天皇|活目入彦五十狹茅尊]]・[[春日神]]を配祀する。 「乙訓坐火雷神」については『[[山城国風土記]]』の[[賀茂神社]]縁起で、賀茂建角身命の子の玉依姫命が、火雷神が化身した丹塗矢によって懐妊し、[[賀茂別雷命]]が生まれたと記されている。賀茂建角身命と玉依姫命は[[賀茂御祖神社|下鴨神社]]の、賀茂別雷命は[[賀茂別雷神社|上賀茂神社]]の祭神である。 == 歴史 == 国史の初見は『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）7月8日条で、その後も[[祈雨]]の神としてたびたび朝廷からの奉幣があった。 [[承久]]3年（[[1221年]]）の[[承久の乱]]で焼失し、長らく復興されないままになっていた。[[文明 (日本)|文明]]16年（[[1484年]]）、現在地に再興され、井ノ内の[[産土神]]とされた。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格した。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} * [https://www.city.nagaokakyo.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5235/hurusatofile41.pdf 角宮神社] - 長岡京市 * [https://kotobank.jp/word/角宮神社-3071841 角宮神社] - コトバンク * [https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamashiro-otokuni-inouchi-suminomiya/ 角宮神社] - 神社巡遊録 {{DEFAULTSORT:すみのみやしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名すみのみやしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:長岡京市の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:長岡京市の建築物]]

Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamayorihime no Mikoto|ja|玉櫛媛}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, and {{ill|Kasuga Kami|ja|春日神}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Sumiyagashira Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture * {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社}} (disputed) * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|ja|玉櫛媛|Tamakushi-hime=Tamakushi-hime|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社}} - Nara Prefecture== See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suminomiya Shrine}}{{citation needed|Suminomiya Shrine}}

Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=draft}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=draft}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=draft}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=draft}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=draft}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Suminomiya Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:角宮神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suminomiya Shrine}}

In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=draft}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=draft}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=draft}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=draft}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=draft}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Suminomiya Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:角宮神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suminomiya Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=draft}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=draft}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=draft}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|ja|玉櫛媛|Tamakushi-hime=Tamakushi-hime|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|12=draft}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|12=draft}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|12=draft}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|12=draft}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=draft}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=draft}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=draft}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=draft}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=draft}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Suminomiya Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:角宮神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suminomiya Shrine}}

In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=draft|12=draft}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=draft|12=draft}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Suminomiya Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suminomiya Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|ja|玉櫛媛|Tamakushi-hime=Tamakushi-hime|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Suminomiya Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suminomiya Shrine}}

revision.comment: 16 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:角宮神社 up to rev 103408474
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年11月10日 (金) 12:13 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=角宮神社 |画像=[[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿（左）・本殿覆屋（右奥） |所在地=[[京都府]][[長岡京市]]井ノ内南内畑35 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[火雷大神|火雷神]] |社格=[[式内社]]（名神大）・旧[[村社]] |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭=[[5月1日]] |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=角宮神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''角宮神社'''（すみのみやじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[長岡京市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[延喜式神名帳|式内]][[名神大社]]「[[山城国]][[乙訓郡]] 乙訓坐大雷神社」の論社である（他の論社は[[向日神社]]（[[向日市]]）に合祀された「火雷神社」）。 == 祭神 == [[火雷大神|火雷神]]（ほのいかづちのかみ）を主[[祭神]]とし、[[玉櫛媛|玉依姫命]]・[[賀茂建角身命|建角身命]]・[[垂仁天皇|活目入彦五十狹茅尊]]・[[春日神]]を配祀する。 「乙訓坐火雷神」については『[[山城国風土記]]』の[[賀茂神社]]縁起で、賀茂建角身命の子の玉依姫命が、火雷神が化身した丹塗矢によって懐妊し、[[賀茂別雷命]]が生まれたと記されている。賀茂建角身命と玉依姫命は[[賀茂御祖神社|下鴨神社]]の、賀茂別雷命は[[賀茂別雷神社|上賀茂神社]]の祭神である。 == 歴史 == 国史の初見は『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）7月8日条で、その後も[[祈雨]]の神としてたびたび朝廷からの奉幣があった。 [[承久]]3年（[[1221年]]）の[[承久の乱]]で焼失し、長らく復興されないままになっていた。[[文明 (日本)|文明]]16年（[[1484年]]）、現在地に再興され、井ノ内の[[産土神]]とされた。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列格した。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} * [https://www.city.nagaokakyo.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5235/hurusatofile41.pdf 角宮神社] - 長岡京市 * [https://kotobank.jp/word/角宮神社-3071841 角宮神社] - コトバンク * [https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamashiro-otokuni-inouchi-suminomiya/ 角宮神社] - 神社巡遊録 {{DEFAULTSORT:すみのみやしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|名すみのみやしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:長岡京市の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:長岡京市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]

Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Suminomiya Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suminomiya Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|ja|玉櫛媛|Tamakushi-hime=Tamakushi-hime|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Suminomiya Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suminomiya Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|ja|玉櫛媛|Tamakushi-hime=Tamakushi-hime|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|ja|玉櫛媛|Tamakushi-hime=Tamakushi-hime|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi no Kami|qq=}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi no Kami|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi no Kami|qq=}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|ja|玉櫛媛|Tamakushi-hime=Tamakushi-hime|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogamo Shrine|qq=}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigamo Shrine|qq=}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi no Kami|qq=}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Honoikazuchi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi no Kami|qq=}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|ja|玉櫛媛|Tamakushi-hime=Tamakushi-hime|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakushi-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂建角身命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|ja|賀茂御祖神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogamo Shrine|qq=}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|ja|賀茂別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigamo Shrine|qq=}}. == History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}} during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi no Kami|ja|火雷大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi no Kami|qq=}} {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]]. == History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]]

In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:長岡京市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Buildings in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]]. == History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]]

In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Suminomiya-jinja}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Nagaokakyo City
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]]. == History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Suminomiya-jinja}}
