It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Suminomiya-jinja}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]]. == History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Suminomiya-jinja}}

Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]]. == History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Suminomiya-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630690]] Wikidata name: Suminomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suminomiya-jinja, shaden.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall (left) and Main Hall Covering (back right) | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto }} '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Engishiki Register of Deities]] for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Muko Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]]. == Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities. Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]]. == History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals. In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine. == Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Muko Shrine)|qq=}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] {{Commonscat|Suminomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|角宮神社|version=103408474|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]]}} [[sn:Suminomiya Shrine]] [[ja:角宮神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] {{Commons category|Suminomiya-jinja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11630690]] Wikidata name: Suminomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|qid=Q861246}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|qq=|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|qq=|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|qq=|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].

Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.

It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630690]] Wikidata name: Suminomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|WD=Q861246}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630690]] Wikidata name: Suminomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|WD=Q861246}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.

It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630690]] Wikidata name: Suminomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|WD=Q861246}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630690]] Wikidata name: Suminomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|WD=Q861246}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.

'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630690]] Wikidata name: Suminomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = [[:en:May 1|May 1]] | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|WD=Q861246}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for [[:en:Yamashiro Province|Yamashiro Province]], Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]] (Fire Thunder God), with [[:en:Tamakushi-hime|Tamayorihime no Mikoto]], [[:en:Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto]], [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of [[:en:Shimogamo Shrine|Shimogamo Shrine]], while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of [[:en:Kamigamo Shrine|Kamigamo Shrine]].
== History == The first historical record appears in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]] during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221), during the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū War]], the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * [[:en:Yakusanoikazuchi|Honoikazuchi no Kami]]

revision.comment: Adding categories based on Wikidata P31: Myōjin Taisha, Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11630690]] Wikidata name: Suminomiya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami}} | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1}} | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|WD=Q861246}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province}}, Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}}.
== History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}} during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū}} 3 (1221), during the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}}, the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] { {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami}} | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1}} | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|WD=Q861246}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province}}, Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine}}.
== History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}} during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū}} 3 (1221), during the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War}}, the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei}} 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Suminomiya-jinja]][[ja:角宮神社]] { {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Suminomiya Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suminomiya Shrine.JPG | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Nagaokakyo City]], Inouchi Minamiuchihata 35 | latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 56 | latitude_sec = 32 | N_or_S = N | longitude_deg = 135 | longitude_min = 41 | longitude_sec = 19.7 | E_or_W = E | map_country_code = JP | deity = {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Myoujin Taisha), Village Shrine | main_hall_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}} | map = Japan Kyoto | location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|WD=Q861246|1=Nagaokakyō, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.94222194444444|N|135.68880611111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}.
== History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}} during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū|1=Jōkyū}} 3 (1221), during the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}}, the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}}
