Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}.
== History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}} during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū|1=Jōkyū}} 3 (1221), during the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}}, the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}}

The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, Otokuni District. '''Suminomiya Shrine''' ({{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Nagaokakyo City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The former [[shrine Rank]] is a [[Village Shrine]]. It is a disputed site for the {{ill|Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|乙訓坐大雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135024354|1=Otokuni-nimasuo Hoikatsuchino Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=Engishiki Register of Deities}} for {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, Otokuni District. The other claim is the {{ill|Mukō Shrine|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō Shrine|qq=}}, which absorbed the "Honoikazuchi Shrine" in [[Muko City]].
== Deities == The primary deity is {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}} (Fire Thunder God), with {{ill|Tamakushi-hime|en|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|lt=Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamakushi-hime}}, {{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and {{ill|Kasuga-no-Kami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} as auxiliary deities.
Regarding "Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine," the [[Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] origin story in the [[Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki]] records that Tamayorihime no Mikoto, daughter of Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto, conceived {{ill|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|ja|賀茂別雷命|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami|2=ja|3=賀茂別雷命|4=zh|5=賀茂別雷命|6=simple|7=Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634943}} through a red lacquered arrow embodiment of Honoikazuchi no Kami. Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto and Tamayorihime no Mikoto are the deities of {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|WD=Q701620|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}, while Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}}.
== History == The first historical record appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}} during the second year of [[:en:Taihō|Taihō]] (702), dated July 8. Subsequently, it received imperial offerings as a deity for rain rituals.
In {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū|1=Jōkyū}} 3 (1221), during the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}}, the shrine was burned down and remained unrestored for an extended period. It was rebuilt in its current location in {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 16 (1484) and became the [[ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Inouchi area. In [[Meiji]] 6 (1873), it was Ranked as a Village Shrine.
== Related Shrines == * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|ja|火雷神社 (玉村町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Tamamura Town)|1=Karai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220848}} - Gunma Prefecture * {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja|火雷神社 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (Gojo City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nara Prefecture * Otokuni Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine - Kyoto Prefecture ** {{ill|Suminomiya Shrine|ja|角宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminomiya Shrine|qq=}} (disputed) ** {{ill|Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|ja|向日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honoikazuchi Shrine (absorbed into Mukō Shrine)|1=Mukō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415986}} (disputed) * {{ill|Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|ja|葛木坐火雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Izasu Honoikazuchi Shrine|1=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620954}} - Nara Prefecture == See Also == * {{ill|Yakusanoikazuchi|en|Yakusanoikazuchi|WD=Q10892534|lt=Honoikazuchi no Kami|1=Yakusanoikazuchi}}
